what_it_does: Defines window.CV_SCAN, an IIFE 'Scanner registry' that builds live indexes of the system's self-knowledge by running registered EXTRACTORS (regexes) over registered SOURCES (lists of {path,text}). Sources resolve files from the live CSSOM, the manifest-referenced files, curated extra app/kit stylesheets, and (when a CV_HOST runtime allows fs/MCP) a real repo tree walk. Extractors pull token usage (var(--x)), @dsCard/@template tags, and TODO/FIXME notes. Runs on load, on an interval, and on tab-focus regain; exposes query helpers (usage/todos/tags) plus describe/stats/report.
observations: scan-index = f(sources × extractors), continuously — the stated organizing equation
registerSource/registerExtractor throw on malformed input (loud), but per-file fetch misses are collected into REPORT.errors not swallowed
run() de-dupes files by path across sources via a seen Set, rebuilds all indexes fresh each run, and only notifies listeners when an index signature changes
Regex execution force-adds the 'g' flag and guards against zero-width-match infinite loops (if m.index === re.lastIndex) re.lastIndex++
CACHE stores content hash+text per path using a djb2-style hash
host-tree source walks the real repo (depth cap 6, skips node_modules/_archive/_ds_bundle/dotfiles) only when CV_HOST.best('list'/'read') exists — dormant in sandbox
cssom source defensively skips cross-origin sheets whose cssRules throw
usage() aggregates a token's hits by file with counts and pulls matching CSSOM selectors (capped at 8/12)
start() polls every 45s only when document is visible; onVis re-runs if stale >10s
standouts: Treats the system's self-knowledge (real token usage graph, tags, TODOs) as a fifth registry built by scanning rather than hand-authored
Self-growing coverage: the manifest source auto-expands as the DS grows, and host-tree gives full repo coverage once exported with file access
conventions: Same register/resolve/query/subscribe registry contract as the other shapes (explicitly the 'fifth-shape registry')
Loud on missing source/extractor; best-effort tolerant on discovery fetches (errors reported, not thrown)
Extend-by-registration: add a source or extractor to scan more / look for more
IIFE + window global pattern shared across atomicity layer
concerns: Regex-based extraction over source text is approximate (e.g. token-usage counts string occurrences, can miss/over-count vs real CSSOM resolution)
fetchUnit hardcodes a '../' path prefix, coupling it to a specific directory layout
extra-styles source carries a hardcoded curated path list that must be maintained by hand
Several empty catch blocks (cross-origin sheets, host read failures) silently drop in a few spots
notes: The self-scanner: register what files to read and what patterns to find, and it keeps live, auto-refreshing indexes of real token usage, specimen/template tags, and open TODOs across the whole project — including the real repo tree when file access is available.
questions: What is CV_HOST and when is it present (export/MCP) to activate the full host-tree coverage?
What consumes the query API (usage/todos/tags) — the inspector, Vi, or a dashboard panel?
Is window.ATLAS.manifest the same manifest referenced elsewhere in the design system?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes a continuously-refreshing scanner registry of self-knowledge with registered sources/extractors; the code implements exactly that, including the loud-vs-best-effort error policy it states.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth extended to discovered (not authored) knowledge
Best-effort discovery with reported, not hidden, failures
intention_signals: Tries to give the design system a live, self-maintaining picture of how it actually uses itself — real token-usage graph, where specimens/templates live, what work is open — without manual rebuilds, scaling from sandbox (CSSOM+manifest) to full repo coverage when file access exists.
novelty: Generalizing the registry pattern to scanned self-knowledge, with a single engine that spans live CSSOM, manifest files, and a real repo walk under one source/extractor model, is the distinct move.
contribution: Contributes the self-knowledge indexing engine: a continuously-refreshing scanner registry that derives the real token-usage graph, specimen/template tags, and open TODOs from sources ranging from live CSSOM to the actual repo tree, with extend-by-registration sources/extractors and a reported (not swallowed) error log.
summary_for_embedding: A JS IIFE (window.CV_SCAN) scanner registry that runs registered regex extractors (token-usage, dscard, template, todo) over registered sources (live CSSOM, manifest files, curated stylesheets, real repo tree via CV_HOST) to keep live, auto-refreshing self-knowledge indexes.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design system a continuously self-maintaining index of how it actually uses itself (real token usage, tags, open work) with no manual rebuild.
